title: drymophloeus communis
intro: Drymophloeus communis, also known as the Camphor tree, is a species of tree that belongs to the family Burseraceae. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its bark, leaves, and roots, which contain camphor, a fragrant, volatile oil. Camphor has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, providing health benefits such as pain relief and improved respiratory function. Herbal preparations of Drymophloeus communis include tea, infusion, and essential oil, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
